---
title: "nimbleModel"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

[Overview of new nimbleModel](overview.html)

[Implementation](implementation.html)

[Graph relationships](graph_relationships.html)

[Index inversion](index_inversion.html)

[Calculation processing](calculation_processing.html)

[Model definition processing](model_processing.html)

[Graph/node querying](graph_querying.html)

[Combining ranges](combining_ranges.html)

Todos (a mostly complete list as of June 2023):

- Core todos:
  - Work on compilation of `calculate` based on `calcRange`. Current approach had declaration-level `calcFun` in each `declRule` that operates on scalar nodes. `calcRangeClass$calculate` iterates over `calcRange`'s original index ranges and calls the `calcFun`.
     - Revisit how to provide vectorized calculations.
  - Consider `simulate`, `getParam`, `getBound`, etc.
  - Incorporate data flags into `getNodes` and/or `getDependencies` using (existing) `dataRule`s.
  - Determine predictive nodes.
  - `calculate` does not yet handle truncation.

- Questions:
  - Do we need `copyRule`s?
  - Do we need `varStore`s?

- Efficiency improvements:
  - Do some benchmarking and comparison to current processing.
    - Consider slowness of building individual samplers for every node in large models.
    - Potential tradeoff of faster creation of graph/modelDef with slower `getDependencies` as that used to be done in C++.
  - Work on combining `varRange`s, in particular when can be done while preserving block `indexRange`s.
  - Revisit handling of state-space style dependence and which cases we do not handle efficiently (worst-case scenario involves very slow recursive unrolling via `fracture`).
  - Avoid duplication of varRanges/nodeRanges/calcRanges (particularly in unrolled cases); e.g., same 'y' deps of intercept and slopes in regression.
  - (long-term) create C++ version of `graphRule`s for run-time dependency resolution.
